title: Guzmania zahnii
intro: Guzmania zahnii, also known as Zahn's Guzmania, is a species of epiphytic bromeliad, a type of flowering plant native to Central and South America. The plant has been found to possess potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as infections and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Guzmania zahnii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Guzmania zahnii include infusions, extracts, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Guzmania zahnii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This family includes over 3,000 species of tropical plants, commonly known as pineapples and air plants. Guzmania zahnii is a type of epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants or surfaces without deriving nutrients from them. The plant is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, where it thrives in humid environments. Guzmania zahnii is further classified as a member of the genus Guzmania, which comprises around 120 species of plants.
